title: cerastium tomentosum var. tomentosum
intro: Cerastium tomentosum var. tomentosum, also known as Snow-in-Summer or Downy Whitlow-grass, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its woolly leaves and white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, skin conditions, and wounds, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cerastium tomentosum var. tomentosum include its ability to soothe sore throats, calm irritated skin, and accelerate wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
